channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Exponential growth but for how long: DeepSeek's meteoric rise in AI-land continues unabated, fresh Similarweb data shows
item.description: Data from Similarweb shows DeepSeek is now the third most used AI tool, but is its popularity already waning?
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Huge healthcare data breach exposes over 1 million Americans' sensitive information
item.description: Community Health Center, a Connecticut-based healthcare provider, has disclosed a recent data breach affecting over 1 million people in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sony PlayStation Network outage enrages gamers around the world
item.description: Sony’s PlayStation Network (PSN) is experiencing a major outage across the world, leaving tens of thousands of gamers unable to access online services, stores and multimedia apps.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Identify any mystery caller for free with this phone number lookup tool
item.description: The TruePeopleSearch.com website works for landline and cellphone numbers, allowing you to do a reverse phone lookup for any type of phone number.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Transportation Department suspends $5 billion EV charger program
item.description: The Federal Highway Administration (FHA) on Thursday announced the suspension of a Biden-era electric vehicle (EV) charging network, the latest in a series of moves by the Trump administration to gut its predecessors’ energy and environmental moves.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: Can’t buy an Nvidia RTX 5080 or 5090 GPU due to stock shortages? If you were hoping GeForce Now might provide a good alternative, we’ve got some bad news
item.description: Nvidia GeForce Now goes from bad to worse for sign-ups, with no plans available at all, now – but day passes will return in a couple of weeks.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Connolly knocks Trump, Musk over 'McCarthy Era 2.0' for government employees
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Thursday knocked President Trump and tech billionaire Elon Musk over what he called a new McCarthy Era for government employees.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Half of C-Suite leaders likely to leave in two years, 27% in six months
item.description: A new Gartner survey of more than 200 C-level executives found 56% are “extremely likely” to quit their current jobs over the next two years — and 27% plan to exit within the next six months.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bay Area food bank reports data breach affecting more than 60,000 people
item.description: The nonprofit has issued a flurry of notices about the hack, would could involve the sensitive information of thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: Gary Klien, The Mercury News

item.title: Hillary Clinton, Duffy tangle over DOGE involvement in Transportation Department
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton and Department of Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy exchanged criticisms online Wednesday over the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) working with various federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Digging into voice AI platform Deepgram
item.description: Deepgram is the leading voice AI platform used by over 200,000 developers to build speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and full speech-to-speech (which enables individuals with speech disabilities to be clearly understood) tools.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Tech layoffs this year: A timeline
item.description: 2025 began in turmoil, with layoffs at some of the largest tech companies despite the support shown by the new US administration. 2024 had been a year of recovery, with the pace of layoffs slowing and IT employment the highest for years following two years of massive IT layoff in 2022 and 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cloud development environments for the win
item.description: About 30 years ago this week, Delphi 1.0 was released. Many of you probably have no idea what Delphi is, but it was a revolutionary rapid application development environment tool. Delphi made developing Windows applications orders of magnitude faster than it had been before.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AMD patches microcode security holes after accidental early disclosure
item.description: AMD on Monday issued two patches for severe microcode security flaws, defects that AMD said “could lead to the loss of Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV) protection.” The bugs were inadvertently revealed by a partner last week.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
